title: Add ids argument to updates field on query
excerpt: There is still no “ids” argument on the “updates” field of a query. We can get this updateid from a trigger (new update created), yet the only way to do anything with it is to turn around and query an item for its most r…

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is still no “ids” argument on the “updates” field of a query. We can get this updateid from a trigger (new update created), yet the only way to do anything with it is to turn around and query an item for its most recent updates.

query ($updateId: [Int!]) {
    updates (ids: $updateId) {
        body,
        text_body,
        assets {
            id,
            name,
            file_size,
            url,
        }
    }
}

{
    "errors": [
        {
            "message": "Field 'updates' doesn't accept argument 'ids'"
        }
    ]
}

Instead the query below is needed, then, item[0].update.map() to get the update in question. This requires getting more updates, and depending on the query more nested queries are required. All of this increases complexity - both query, and code.

query ($itemId: [Int!]) {
    items (ids: $itemId) {
        updates (limit: 5) {
            id,
            assets {
                id,
                name,
                url
            }
        }
    }
}

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @anon29275264!

Thank you for the feedback. I have shared it with the team

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: Shanee (Shanee Radzewsky)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just updating that this is now possible as of the previous version
See documentation

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Documentation makes no mention of update ID being an argument for querying updates. Though it does appear in the schema now. Good to know.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: @dipro or @Matias.Monday this one is long released.

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Good catch. Here’s the relevant documentation for this field & argument:

monday.com Platform API

Updates

Learn how to query monday.com board or item updates using the platform API
